title: Improve gated clock support in hierarhical verilation
body:
If a hierarchical block creates a gated clock inside it, the simulation result may be wrong. (It took several days to figure out).

Thanks to #2172, just marking the original clock as `clocker` is enough.

Here are some random ideas to improve.
- Warn if clock is gated inside a hierarchical block and advise to add `--clk` option
- Set `--clk` option when Verilating a hierarchical block if `--clk` is set to the top module
  - When Verilating a hierarchical block `sub` as below,  `--clk subclk` must be set, `--clk clk` is not correct.


```systemverilog
module top(input clk /*verilator clocker*/);
  wire subclk = clk;
  sub i_sub(.subclk(subclk));
endmodule

module sub(input subclk); /* verilator hier_block*/
  wire gated_clk = subclk | some_gate_ctrl_signal;
  always_ff @(posedge gated_clk) ....
endmodule
```
